title: Sequential vs Parallel
description: Read multiple files using async/await, first sequentially then in parallel.
problemStatement:
You have three files: `a.txt`, `b.txt`, `c.txt`. Each contains a line of text. Write an async function `readSequential` that reads them one after another (using `await`) and logs the combined content. Then write another async function `readParallel` that reads them in parallel using `Promise.all` and logs the combined content. Measure the time difference (you can use `console.time` and `console.timeEnd`).

Assume you have `fs.promises` available.

Create the files manually or simulate with `setTimeout` if you prefer.
hints: For sequential: `const a = await fs.readFile('a.txt');` etc.
For parallel: `const [a, b, c] = await Promise.all([fs.readFile('a.txt'), ...])`.
expectedOutput: Combined content of all files (order may vary for parallel but you can sort).
